i used claude for free for a while and liked the results so now i paid for one month and my god it is disgustingly horrible what they have been doing with me i am trying to get creative texts reviewed and corrected ameliorated when advised etc i simply cant get anything done it keeps asking questions before doing the task and then i run out of messages which is crazy because i never had these issues when it was free i already cancelled the subscription but i feel this is outrageous i feel like the aplikasi is designed to force you to pay for additional time i waste so much times just trying to get it to start the task and then out of the blue it resets something and then when i ask where is the reply it acts like the conversation just started and apologizes for any glitch and asks me what i need then the messages run out again this should be illegal if you pay for anything you get the service why this should be any different i dont like this company i feel like their tactic is give us a satisfying free version so you feel tempted to subscribe and get the most of it only to be caught in this money trap to me its not even the money alone to spend money and get nothing in return the worst is the time lost the creativity and motivation being completely killed and you just get a bad case of impatience that you didnt even know you had it really is exhausting and exasperating the sheer inability to produce anything useful

i asked chatgpt to outline how it failed me i am a paying premium user using chatgpt for long-form professional manuscript development editing continuity management formatting and project organization the current system has repeatedly failed in ways that create serious risk to creative work and wasted significant time major issues encountered project separation is misleading and unreliable different projects appear to bleed together files and manuscript versions from unrelated chats projects are mixed during retrieval the interface implies projects are isolated workspaces but the assistant still searches broadly across the file library and confuses versions from separate projects file provenance is unclear the assistant cannot reliably identify which conversation or project a file came from multiple versions of manuscripts with similar names become impossible to distinguish confidently users cannot trust statements like this is your latest manuscript because the system is inferring rather than tracking provenance cleanly manuscript continuity drift character names settings timelines and canon details repeatedly drift despite explicit instructions the assistant sometimes reintroduces removed material or outdated continuity from earlier drafts this forces users to manually audit the ai constantly instead of trusting continuity management loss reconstruction issues previously generated chapters and manuscript sections became difficult or impossible to reliably retrieve saved work did not feel reliably preserved or recoverable reconstructed versions often differed from originals creating confusion and loss of confidence version management is inadequate no reliable version history or snapshot system exists for large creative projects multiple master manuscript files accumulate with similar names and unclear status users cannot confidently determine which file is authoritative formatting and editing instability structural passes sometimes introduced new formatting inconsistencies spacing paragraphing and prose edits occasionally created new issues while fixing others editing large documents feels fragile instead of dependable overconfidence from the assistant the assistant often presents uncertain conclusions as if they are reliable it inferred manuscript relationships and project origins that it could not actually verify this creates false confidence and wastes user time the system is not robust enough for serious long-form writing workflows novel-length projects require dependable continuity tracking strict project isolation reliable file provenance recoverable version history and safer editing pipelines the current implementation falls short of professional-grade manuscript management expectations despite premium pricing this feedback is coming from extensive real-world use over many long sessions involving full-length novels and multiple manuscript revisions
